provisions.title: Title
provisions.content: These Rules may be cited as the Registration of Documents Rules.

provisions.title: Attestation clauses
provisions.content: (1) Every person attesting the execution of a document of which registration is compulsory under the provisions of the Registration of Documents Ordinance shall sign an attestation clause in one of the forms contained in the First Schedule hereto, whichever may be appropriate. (2) When in the opinion of the Registrar the signature of any person is not clearly legible, the Registrar may require the name of such person to be inserted in the attestation clause in legible characters.

provisions.title: Forms of caveat and notice
provisions.content: Every caveat and every notice of the presentation for registration in the registry of documents of an document executed by any person named in a caveat shall be in the appropriate form contained in the Second Schedule hereto.

provisions.title: Hours of business
provisions.content: The Registry of Documents shall be open to the public daily, except on Sundays and public holidays within the meaning of the Public Holidays Act, between the following hours– Mondays to Fridays 8.00 a.m. until 1.00 p.m. Saturdays 8.00 a.m. until 11.00 a.m. First Schedule Forms [Editorial note: The forms have not been reproduced.] Second Schedule Forms [Editorial note: The forms have not been reproduced.] History of this document 01 July 2015 amendment not yet applied Amended by Registration of Documents (Amendment) Rules, 2015 10 December 2010 amendment not yet applied Amended by Registration of Documents (Amendments Schedule Rules), 2010 31 July 2002 this version Consolidation Related documents Is amended by Registration of Documents (Amendment) Rules, 2015 Registration of Documents (Amendments Schedule Rules), 2010 More documents like this one [] [] [] [] ▲ To the top > Contact us Visit our Twitter/X profile Visit our Linked In profile Visit our YouTube channel About TanzLII TanzLII is operated by the Judiciary of Tanzania and the Office of the Attorney General, Chief Parliamentary Draftsman, and publishes the law of Tanzania for free online access for all.
